title: Quantum Illumination — entanglement-enhanced target detection
sub_domain: Two-mode squeezing for target detection below shot-noise
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical literature for this principle.

E.description: Quantum Illumination — entanglement-enhanced target detection: entanglement detection produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.entangled_photon_source -> L.signal_probe_return -> L.joint_detection -> int.temporal, with time-integrated exposure and photon-shot-noise-limited (Poisson counting). Recovery is posed as a non-convex inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side binary detection map. Difficulty tier delta=10 with effective condition number kappa_eff~35; calibration-level mismatch (loss_and_noise_decoherence, phase_drift, mode_mismatch) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: P_detect(target) via joint measurement of idler and return; 6 dB advantage over classical at low transmissivity

W.regime: Existence of the recovered binary detection map is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness is local rather than global (non-convex landscape); convergence depends on initialisation and priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 35); loss_and_noise_decoherence dominates the stability cliff; phase_drift and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Photon-shot-noise-limited (poisson counting) sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while TV / wavelet-sparsity / deep priors stabilise recovery at the ill-conditioned end of Omega.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [Sum-Frequency-Gen, Optical-Parametric] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [QI-Decoder-Net]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sqrt(sigma_signal^2) / sqrt(photon_count). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |loss_and_noise_decoherence| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
